Are the reported issues present in the code?**  \n- The code is a standard autogenerated ethers.js factory class for deploying and interacting with an Ethereum smart contract named 'Permit2'.  \n- It contains static ABI and bytecode, and methods for deploy, attach, connect, and interface creation.  \n- No dynamic or runtime data processing, no external network calls, and no suspicious code segments are present.  \n- The ABI details, bytecode, and factory pattern are typical for such deployment scripts.\n\n**Conclusion:** The reports' claims about potential untrusted data flows or malicious activity are unfounded. The code does not process user input beyond standard parameters for deployment or connection, and these are handled safely within the ethers.js framework.\n\n---\n\n**2.
